name: Ohio DRC — Office of Reentry
description: The Ohio Department of Rehabilitation and Correction Office of Reentry coordinates statewide reentry planning and connects people in custody or under community supervision with local coalitions, housing, employment, treatment, and legal resources. Staff support pre-release planning and community partner navigation across Ohio. This office provides planning and referrals—not emergency crisis response.
description_es: La Oficina de Reinserción del Departamento de Rehabilitación y Corrección de Ohio coordina la planificación estatal y conecta a personas en custodia o bajo supervisión comunitaria con coaliciones locales, vivienda, empleo, tratamiento y recursos legales. El personal apoya la planificación previa a la liberación y la navegación de aliados comunitarios en todo Ohio. Esta oficina ofrece planificación y referencias, no respuesta de crisis.
eligibility: Generally serves Ohio residents under DRC custody, parole, or seeking state reentry coordination and coalition referrals.
eligibility_es: Generalmente sirve a residentes de Ohio bajo custodia del DRC, libertad condicional o que buscan coordinación estatal de reinserción.
notes: Contact via phone or email; see drc.ohio.gov for regional reentry coalition listings. Not a walk-in crisis line.
notes_es: Contacte por teléfono o correo; consulte drc.ohio.gov para coaliciones regionales. No es una línea de crisis.
services: Reentry planning coordination|Regional coalition referrals|Pre-release resource navigation|Community partner connections

name: Relink.org
description: Relink.org is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Free statewide online directory connecting people in addiction recovery, human trafficking recovery, and incarceration reentry with local service providers. Users search by location and service type for housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, and peer support. Relink is a referral hub—it does not provide direct services. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
description_es: Relink.org es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para ohianos afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en en todo Ohio. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
eligibility: Open to Ohio residents seeking reentry, recovery, or anti-trafficking resources; no criminal-record restrictions stated.
eligibility_es: Abierto a residentes de Ohio que buscan recursos de reinserción, recuperación o anti-trata; sin restricciones de antecedentes indicadas.
notes: Search online anytime; phone support available during business hours for navigation help.
notes_es: Busque en línea en cualquier momento; soporte telefónico en horario laboral para ayuda de navegación.
hours: Available online 24/7; phone support Monday–Friday business hours
services: Online resource directory|Geo-located provider search|Reentry service navigation|Recovery resource referrals

name: Ohio Association of Local Reentry Coalitions (OALRC)
description: Ohio Association of Local Reentry Coalitions (OALRC) is a regional reentry coalition serving throughout Ohio. Partner agencies, faith communities, and workforce organizations meet regularly to share resources and warm referrals for people leaving jail or prison, on probation or parole, and their families. Coordinators help returning citizens connect to housing, employment, treatment, legal aid, and benefits through local partners. This is primarily a networking and navigation council—not an emergency shelter, cash assistance program, or crisis line.
description_es: Ohio Association of Local Reentry Coalitions (OALRC) es una coalición regional de reinserción que sirve en todo Ohio. Agencias aliadas, comunidades de fe y organizaciones laborales se reúnen para compartir recursos y referencias directas para personas que salen de la cárcel o prisión, en libertad condicional o vigilada, y sus familias. Los coordinadores ayudan a conectar a ciudadanos que regresan con vivienda, empleo, tratamiento, asistencia legal y beneficios a través de aliados locales. Es principalmente un consejo de red y navegación—no un refugio de emergencia, programa de efectivo ni línea de crisis.
eligibility: Open to Ohio reentry coalitions, community partners, and justice-involved individuals seeking local coalition contacts.
eligibility_es: Abierto a coaliciones de reinserción de Ohio, aliados comunitarios y personas buscando contactos locales.
notes: Contact president Tom Luettke for coalition information; see ODRC reentry coalition PDF for local contacts.
notes_es: Contacte al presidente para información de coaliciones; consulte el PDF de coaliciones del DRC para contactos locales.
hours: Contact for current hours; confirm before visiting
services: Coalition network coordination|Advocacy|Resource sharing|Local coalition referrals

name: Ohio 211
description: Ohio 211 is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Free statewide information and referral service connecting Ohioans to health and human services including housing, food, utilities, employment, and crisis support. Specialists help callers find local programs by need and location. 211 is a referral line—not a direct-service provider. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
description_es: Ohio 211 es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para ohianos afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en en todo Ohio. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
eligibility: Open to all Ohio residents; no criminal-record restrictions stated.
eligibility_es: Abierto a todos los residentes de Ohio; sin restricciones de antecedentes indicadas.
notes: Dial 211 from any Ohio phone; text your ZIP code to 898211; chat available at 211.org.
notes_es: Marque 211 desde cualquier teléfono de Ohio; envíe su código postal por texto al 898211.
services: Information and referral|Housing resource navigation|Benefits referrals|Crisis resource connections

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — Ohio
description: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — Ohio serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. Free confidential 24/7 crisis support for people experiencing mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, or substance use crises. Trained counselors provide immediate support and can connect callers to local crisis services. Available to anyone in Ohio—not reentry-specific but essential for justice-involved individuals in crisis. Services are available on a sliding fee or Medicaid basis for eligible patients; bring ID and release paperwork when establishing care after incarceration. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — Ohio sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en en todo Ohio. Los servicios están disponibles con tarifa móvil o Medicaid para pacientes elegibles; lleve identificación y documentos de liberación al establecer atención después de la encarcelación. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Open to anyone in Ohio experiencing a mental health or suicide crisis; no eligibility restrictions.
eligibility_es: Abierto a cualquier persona en Ohio en crisis de salud mental o suicidio; sin restricciones.
notes: Call or text 988; Spanish-language support available. For immediate danger call 911.
notes_es: Llame o envíe texto al 988; soporte en español disponible. Para peligro inmediato llame al 911.
services: Crisis counseling|Suicide prevention support|Mental health referrals|Substance use crisis support

name: Ohio Legal Help
description: Ohio Legal Help is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Statewide legal information portal providing plain-language guides, court forms, and tools for sealing criminal records, housing, benefits, and family law. Helps Ohioans find local legal aid providers and understand their rights. Online resource hub—not a law firm providing direct representation on its own. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
description_es: Ohio Legal Help es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para ohianos afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en en todo Ohio. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
eligibility: Open to all Ohio residents seeking legal information; direct representation eligibility varies by local legal aid provider.
eligibility_es: Abierto a todos los residentes de Ohio que buscan información legal; la representación directa varía según la organización local.
notes: Call 1-866-LAW-OHIO (529-6446) for help finding legal resources; website available in English and Spanish.
notes_es: Llame al 1-866-LAW-OHIO (529-6446) para encontrar recursos legales; sitio web en inglés y español.
hours: Phone line Monday–Friday, 8:30 a.m.–4:30 p.m. ET; website 24/7
services: Legal information guides|Court form assistance|Local legal aid finder|Record sealing information

Provides navigation and referrals to local reentry programs and partner agencies across the state. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

eligibility_es: Residentes de Ohio que cumplan las reglas del programa para Ohio Reentry Connections; los antecedentes penales generalmente no son barrera para información y referencias.
notes: Visit website for resource listings and partner contacts; verify current phone intake on site.
notes_es: Visite el sitio web para listados de recursos y contactos de aliados.

name: Certificate of Qualification for Employment (CQE) — Ohio DRC
description: Certificate of Qualification for Employment (CQE) — Ohio DRC serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. Ohio's Certificate of Qualification for Employment program helps eligible individuals with criminal records obtain a certificate that can reduce automatic licensing and employment barriers. Administered through the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction with application guidance at drccqe.com. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: Certificate of Qualification for Employment (CQE) — Ohio DRC ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en en todo Ohio. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Ohio residents with qualifying criminal records seeking employment or occupational licensing relief; offense-type and waiting-period rules apply.
eligibility_es: Residentes de Ohio con antecedentes penales elegibles que buscan alivio para empleo o licencias; aplican reglas de tipo de delito y períodos de espera.
notes: Apply online at drccqe.com; legal aid and reentry partners can assist with applications.
notes_es: Solicite en línea en drccqe.com; aliados legales y de reinserción pueden ayudar con las solicitudes.
hours: Online application available 24/7; DRC office Monday–Friday business hours
services: CQE application processing|Employment barrier relief|Occupational licensing guidance

name: Ohio Adult Parole Authority
description: Ohio Adult Parole Authority serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. State agency responsible for parole supervision, parole board decisions, and community supervision of individuals released from Ohio prisons. Parole officers connect supervisees to reentry resources and enforce supervision conditions. Contact for supervision status, reporting requirements, and regional office locations. This office handles supervision compliance and reporting—not emergency reentry navigation; ask your officer for referrals to local reentry partners. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: Ohio Adult Parole Authority sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en en todo Ohio. Esta oficina maneja cumplimiento de supervisión e informes—no navegación de crisis de reinserción; pida a su oficial referencias a aliados locales de reinserción. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Individuals under Ohio parole or community supervision; family members may contact for general information.
eligibility_es: Personas bajo libertad condicional o supervisión comunitaria de Ohio; familiares pueden contactar para información general.
notes: Regional parole offices statewide; not a reentry resource navigation line—contact Office of Reentry for resource referrals.
notes_es: Oficinas regionales en todo el estado; para referencias de recursos contacte la Oficina de Reinserción.
services: Parole supervision|Community supervision|Parole board services|Supervision compliance support

name: Ohio Benefits — benefits.ohio.gov
description: Ohio Benefits — benefits.ohio.gov is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Official statewide online portal for applying for and managing Ohio public benefits including Medicaid, SNAP (food assistance), cash assistance, and child care subsidies. Justice-involved individuals can apply for coverage and food support after release. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
eligibility: Ohio residents meeting income and program requirements for Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, or child care; criminal record generally not a barrier.
eligibility_es: Residentes de Ohio que cumplan requisitos de ingresos para Medicaid, SNAP, TANF o cuidado infantil; antecedentes penales generalmente no son barrera.
notes: Create account online; county Job and Family Services offices assist with applications and document verification.
notes_es: Cree una cuenta en línea; las oficinas del condado de Job and Family Services ayudan con solicitudes.
services: Medicaid application|SNAP enrollment|Cash assistance application|Benefits account management

name: OhioMHAS Consumer & Family Toll-Free Bridge Line
description: OhioMHAS Consumer & Family Toll-Free Bridge Line is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Free statewide phone line staffed by peer specialists providing referral information for addiction and mental health services, Medicaid navigation, and consumer rights guidance. Helps Ohioans find treatment providers and understand how to access behavioral health care. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

notes: Also reachable locally at (614) 466-7228; email askMHAS@mha.ohio.gov for non-urgent questions.
notes_es: También disponible localmente al (614) 466-7228; correo askMHAS@mha.ohio.gov para preguntas no urgentes.
services: Treatment referrals|Mental health resource navigation|Medicaid information|Consumer rights guidance

name: ODJFS — Restored Citizens Initiative
description: ODJFS — Restored Citizens Initiative serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. Ohio Department of Job and Family Services initiative helping restored citizens (formerly incarcerated individuals) access public benefits, employment services, and community reintegration support upon release. Coordinates with county JFS offices and reentry partners statewide. Bring proof of identity, income, and release or residency documents when applying at a county office or online portal. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: ODJFS — Restored Citizens Initiative sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en en todo Ohio. Lleve prueba de identidad, ingresos y documentos de liberación o residencia al solicitar en una oficina del condado o portal en línea. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Ohio residents recently released from incarceration seeking benefits enrollment and employment support.
eligibility_es: Residentes de Ohio recién liberados de prisión que buscan inscripción en beneficios y apoyo de empleo.
notes: Contact local county Job and Family Services office for intake; bring release documents and ID.
notes_es: Contacte la oficina local de Job and Family Services; traiga documentos de liberación e identificación.
hours: County office hours vary; state office Monday–Friday business hours
services: Benefits enrollment assistance|Employment services referral|Reentry benefits navigation

State-supported online directory of certified recovery housing in Ohio for people in substance use recovery, including options that accept justice-involved referrals. Users search by county and housing level for sober living and recovery residences. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

name: OhioMHAS Access to Recovery (ATR)
description: OhioMHAS Access to Recovery (ATR) serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 9 Ohio counties. Ohio Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services voucher program supporting criminal justice-involved adults and veterans in recovery with services including recovery housing, transportation, employment training, and peer support. Available in select counties through participating care coordinators. Treatment may require assessment, insurance or Medicaid enrollment, and referral from courts or corrections for some beds—call intake to confirm openings. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: OhioMHAS Access to Recovery (ATR) sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en 9 condados de Ohio. El tratamiento puede requerir evaluación, seguro o Medicaid y referencia de tribunales o correcciones para algunas plazas—llame a admisión para confirmar disponibilidad. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Criminal justice-involved adults and veterans in recovery in participating counties: Columbiana, Cuyahoga, Franklin, Hamilton, Lorain, Mahoning, Montgomery, Stark, and Summit.
eligibility_es: Adultos con antecedentes penales y veteranos en recuperación en condados participantes: Columbiana, Cuyahoga, Franklin, Hamilton, Lorain, Mahoning, Montgomery, Stark y Summit.
notes: Contact an ATR care coordinator at a participating agency in your county; see OhioMHAS page for provider list.
notes_es: Contacte un coordinador ATR en una agencia participante de su condado; consulte la página de OhioMHAS.
services: Recovery housing vouchers|Transportation assistance|Employment skills training|Peer recovery support

Free confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information service for individuals and families facing mental health or substance use disorders. Provides referrals to local treatment facilities and community organizations in Ohio and nationwide. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

name: FindTreatment.gov — Ohio Provider Search
description: FindTreatment.gov — Ohio Provider Search serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. Official SAMHSA online locator for substance use and mental health treatment facilities serving Ohio. Users search by location, treatment type, and payment options to find licensed providers with current openings. Treatment may require assessment, insurance or Medicaid enrollment, and referral from courts or corrections for some beds—call intake to confirm openings. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.

name: Clark County Department of Reentry
description: Clark County Department of Reentry is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. County reentry department coordinating services for justice-involved Clark County residents including referrals to housing, employment, treatment, and ID assistance. Works with the Clark County Reentry Coalition and community partners. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in Clark County. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
description_es: Clark County Department of Reentry es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para ohianos afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en condado de Clark. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
eligibility: Clark County residents who are justice-involved or recently released; contact for intake requirements.
eligibility_es: Residentes de Clark con antecedentes penales o recién liberados; contacte para requisitos.
notes: Contact Brooke Wagner for intake; coalition email reentry@clarkcountyohio.gov.
notes_es: Contacte a Brooke Wagner para admisión.
services: Reentry case coordination|Housing referrals|Employment referrals|ID assistance referrals

name: Legal Aid Society of Cleveland
description: Legal Aid Society of Cleveland serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 5 Ohio counties. Provides free civil legal aid including record sealing, housing, benefits, and employment issues for low-income residents in five northeast Ohio counties. Income and household-size limits apply. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: Legal Aid Society of Cleveland ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en 5 condados de Ohio. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Low-income Ohio residents in service area; veterans and seniors may qualify with higher income limits. Criminal record relief eligibility varies by offense.
eligibility_es: Residentes de bajos ingresos en el área de servicio; veteranos y personas mayores pueden calificar. La elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes varía.
notes: Apply online or call intake line; record sealing clinics offered in many counties.
notes_es: Solicite en línea o llame a la línea de admisión; clínicas de sellado de antecedentes en muchos condados.
hours: Intake Monday–Friday mornings; call for appointment
services: Record sealing assistance|Expungement help|Housing legal aid|Benefits advocacy|Civil legal representation

name: Ohio Ex-Offender Reentry Coalition (via OALRC)
description: Ohio Ex-Offender Reentry Coalition (via OALRC) is a regional reentry coalition serving throughout Ohio. Partner agencies, faith communities, and workforce organizations meet regularly to share resources and warm referrals for people leaving jail or prison, on probation or parole, and their families. Coordinators help returning citizens connect to housing, employment, treatment, legal aid, and benefits through local partners. This is primarily a networking and navigation council—not an emergency shelter, cash assistance program, or crisis line.
description_es: Ohio Ex-Offender Reentry Coalition (via OALRC) es una coalición regional de reinserción que sirve en todo Ohio. Agencias aliadas, comunidades de fe y organizaciones laborales se reúnen para compartir recursos y referencias directas para personas que salen de la cárcel o prisión, en libertad condicional o vigilada, y sus familias. Los coordinadores ayudan a conectar a ciudadanos que regresan con vivienda, empleo, tratamiento, asistencia legal y beneficios a través de aliados locales. Es principalmente un consejo de red y navegación—no un refugio de emergencia, programa de efectivo ni línea de crisis.

name: Akron-Canton Regional Foodbank
description: Akron-Canton Regional Foodbank serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 6 Ohio counties. Regional food bank serving Summit, Stark, Portage, and surrounding counties connecting low-income residents including returning citizens to food pantries, mobile distributions, and SNAP application assistance. Food assistance does not usually require a criminal-record disclosure; staff can help with SNAP applications and pantry locations by ZIP code. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: Akron-Canton Regional Foodbank sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en 6 condados de Ohio. La asistencia alimentaria generalmente no requiere declarar antecedentes penales; el personal puede ayudar con solicitudes SNAP y ubicaciones de despensas por código postal. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Low-income residents in service area; access food through partner pantries and programs.
eligibility_es: Residentes de bajos ingresos en el área de servicio; acceda a alimentos a través de despensas aliadas y programas.
notes: Use food bank website pantry locator; does not distribute food directly to walk-in individuals at warehouse.
notes_es: Use el localizador de despensas en el sitio web; no distribuye alimentos directamente sin cita en el almacén.
services: Food pantry network|Mobile food distributions|SNAP application assistance|Partner agency referrals

name: Ohio State Moritz College of Law — Drug Enforcement and Policy Center Clinic
description: Ohio State Moritz College of Law — Drug Enforcement and Policy Center Clinic is a statewide or regional referral resource for Ohioans affected by the criminal justice system. Ohio State University law clinic assisting eligible clients with criminal record sealing, collateral consequences relief, and reentry legal issues through Opportunity Port partner referrals and direct clinic intake. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Ohio. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

name: Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation — Background Check Services
description: Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation — Background Check Services serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Ohio. Ohio Attorney General BCI office providing criminal background checks and fingerprinting services needed for employment, licensing, and legal record review by individuals with criminal records seeking reentry. State offices provide information and referrals statewide; county and nonprofit partners deliver most direct services. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
description_es: Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation — Background Check Services sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en en todo Ohio. Las oficinas estatales ofrecen información y referencias en todo el estado; socios del condado y sin fines de lucro prestan la mayoría de servicios directos. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.
eligibility: Ohio residents and employers requesting BCI background checks; individuals may request own record review.
eligibility_es: Residentes de Ohio y empleadores que solicitan verificaciones BCI; las personas pueden solicitar revisión de su propio registro.
notes: WebCheck locations statewide for fingerprinting; needed for many jobs and expungement preparation.
notes_es: Ubicaciones WebCheck en todo el estado para huellas dactilares; necesario para muchos empleos y preparación de eliminación de antecedentes.
services: Background checks|Fingerprinting|Record review|WebCheck services
